title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 11: Advanced Problems
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 408 - 175 + 62?
A rectangle has length 9 cm and width 4 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='40'/><text x='55' y='10' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>9 cm</text><text x='108' y='40' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Rectangle with length 9 cm and width 4 cm.

questions.question_text: Which of these numbers is divisible by both 3 and 4?
questions.solution: A number divisible by 3 and 4 is divisible by 12. Only 24 = 12 × 2 fits.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ... each term follows a rule. What is the next term?
questions.solution: Each term is double the previous plus 1: 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.question_text: A movie started at 14:35 and ended at 16:20. How long was it?
questions.solution: From 14:35 to 16:35 is 2 hours; subtract 15 min to reach 16:20. Total = 1 h 45 min.

questions.question_text: Four friends sit in a row. Ana is not at either end. Ben sits next to Ana. Cara sits at the left end. Dan sits at the right end. Who is second from the left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='22'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='40' height='22'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='40' height='22'/><rect x='145' y='10' width='40' height='22'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four empty seats in a row.
questions.solution: Cara is in seat 1, Dan in seat 4. Ana must be in seat 2 or 3. Since Ben must be next to Ana and only one middle seat remains, the arrangement is Cara, Ana, Ben, Dan. Second from left is Ana.

questions.question_text: What is 15% of 240?
A square has area 49 square centimeters. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 80 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='15' width='50' height='50'/></svg>
questions.solution: Side = sqrt(49) = 7 cm. Perimeter = 4 × 7 = 28 cm.

questions.question_text: In a jar, the ratio of red to blue marbles is 3:5. If there are 15 red marbles, how many blue marbles are there?
questions.solution: 15 red means each part = 5 marbles. Blue = 5 × 5 = 25.

questions.question_text: Jane has 3 shirts and 4 pairs of pants. How many different shirt-pants outfits can she make?
questions.solution: 3 × 4 = 12 combinations.

questions.question_text: If 3x + 7 = 31, what is the value of x?
A triangle has two sides of lengths 5 cm and 8 cm. Which of the following could NOT be the length of the third side?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,65 100,65 60,15'/></svg>
questions.solution: Triangle inequality: third side must be between 8-5=3 and 8+5=13. So 14 cm is impossible.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all positive divisors of 20?
Look at the pattern of dots: row 1 has 1 dot, row 2 has 4 dots, row 3 has 9 dots, row 4 has 16 dots. How many dots are in row 7?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='currentColor' stroke-width='1'><circle cx='10' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='30' cy='20' r='3'/><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='40' cy='20' r='3'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='60' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='60' cy='45' r='3'/><circle cx='70' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='70' cy='45' r='3'/><circle cx='80' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='80' cy='45' r='3'/></svg>
questions.solution: Row n has n² dots. Row 7 = 49.

questions.question_text: A water tank holds 48 liters when full. It is currently 3/8 full. How many more liters are needed to fill it completely?
questions.solution: Currently 3/8 × 48 = 18 L. Need 48 - 18 = 30 L more.

questions.question_text: In a class, every student studies at least one of math or science. 18 study math, 15 study science, and 7 study both. How many students are in the class?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='55' cy='40' r='30'/><circle cx='85' cy='40' r='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two overlapping circles representing math and science.
questions.solution: Using inclusion-exclusion: 18 + 15 - 7 = 26 students.

questions.question_text: The average of five numbers is 12. When one number is removed, the average of the remaining four becomes 11. What was the removed number?
questions.solution: Total of five = 60. Total of four = 44. Removed = 60 - 44 = 16.

questions.question_text: A regular hexagon is divided into how many equilateral triangles when all long diagonals from the center are drawn?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='50,10 85,30 85,70 50,90 15,70 15,30'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90'/><line x1='85' y1='30' x2='15' y2='70'/><line x1='15' y1='30' x2='85' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Regular hexagon divided by three long diagonals through the center.
questions.solution: Three long diagonals meet at the center, dividing the hexagon into 6 equilateral triangles.

questions.question_text: A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio 7:2. If 28 cups of flour are used, how many total cups of flour and sugar are mixed together?
questions.solution: 28 cups flour means each part = 4. Sugar = 2 × 4 = 8. Total = 28 + 8 = 36.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 1, 2, 3, 4 if no digit is repeated?
questions.solution: 4 × 3 × 2 = 24 arrangements.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive odd integers is 87. What is the largest of them?
questions.solution: Middle = 87/3 = 29. The three consecutive odd integers are 27, 29, 31. The largest is 31.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is 12 cm by 8 cm. A square of side 3 cm is cut from one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,20 130,20 130,90 10,90 10,50 40,50 40,20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: L-shaped figure: 12 by 8 rectangle with a 3 by 3 square removed.
questions.solution: Total = 12 × 8 = 96. Remove 3 × 3 = 9. Remaining = 96 - 9 = 87 cm².

questions.question_text: What is the smallest positive integer that leaves a remainder of 2 when divided by 3, 4, and 5?
questions.solution: Need n ≡ 2 (mod LCM(3,4,5)=60). Smallest n > 0: n = 62.

questions.question_text: Three boxes contain apples, oranges, or a mix of both. Every box is labeled incorrectly. You pick one fruit from the box labeled 'Mix' and it is an apple. What does the box labeled 'Mix' actually contain?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='5' y='15' width='40' height='35'/><rect x='60' y='15' width='40' height='35'/><rect x='115' y='15' width='40' height='35'/></svg>
questions.solution: Since every label is wrong, the 'Mix' box is not mix. You drew an apple, so it must be apples only.

questions.question_text: If today is Wednesday, what day of the week will it be 100 days from today?
questions.solution: 100 ÷ 7 = 14 remainder 2. Two days after Wednesday is Friday.

questions.question_text: Two trains leave the same station in opposite directions. One travels at 60 km/h and the other at 80 km/h. After how many hours are they 350 km apart?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><line x1='10' y1='20' x2='190' y2='20'/><rect x='85' y='10' width='30' height='20'/><polyline points='70,15 80,20 70,25'/><polyline points='130,15 120,20 130,25'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A station with arrows pointing in opposite directions.
questions.solution: Combined speed = 140 km/h. Time = 350 / 140 = 2.5 hours.

questions.question_text: From a group of 4 boys and 3 girls, a committee of 2 boys and 2 girls is to be formed. How many different committees are possible?
questions.solution: Choose 2 boys from 4: C(4,2) = 6. Choose 2 girls from 3: C(3,2) = 3. Total = 6 × 3 = 18.

questions.question_text: A cube has a surface area of 96 square centimeters. What is its volume?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 90 90' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='25' width='50' height='50'/><polyline points='15,25 30,10 80,10 80,60 65,75'/><line x1='65' y1='25' x2='80' y2='10'/></svg>
questions.solution: 6a² = 96, so a² = 16, a = 4. Volume = 4³ = 64 cm³.

questions.question_text: A shirt is marked up by 20%, then discounted by 20% from the new price. If the original price was $50, what is the final price?
The sum of two numbers is 45 and their difference is 13. What is the smaller number?
questions.solution: Let numbers be x and y with x > y. x + y = 45, x - y = 13. Adding: 2x = 58, x = 29. y = 45 - 29 = 16.
